title: Intensely Mustardy Russian Sevaiiya Noodles
description: A pescatarian-friendly Russian dish featuring pillowy sevaiiya noodles in a rich, slow-cooked mustard sauce, perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: sevaiiya noodles
ingredients.notes: available at Russian or Middle Eastern markets

instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the sevaiiya noodles according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and cook until fragrant.
Add the dijon mustard, whole grain mustard, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mustard sauce for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the chopped fresh dill and cook for an additional minute.
Add the canned salmon to the skillet and stir to combine with the mustard sauce.
Add the cooked sevaiiya noodles to the skillet and toss to combine with the salmon and mustard sauce.
In a small bowl, whisk together the chicken broth and sour cream, then stir into the noodles and sauce.
Simmer the noodles and sauce over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the noodles are well coated.
Serve the intensely mustardy Russian sevaiiya noodles hot, garnished with additional chopped fresh dill if desired.
